scenarios.name: feature-commit-with-why
scenarios.prompt: Write the git commit message for this change. I changed the oracleImage() helper to detect Oracle migration scenarios with strings.Contains instead of a hardcoded enum of known scenario names. I did it this way so that future Oracle scenarios are covered automatically without anyone having to update the enum. Scope it to ticket MIG-7276.

assertions.text: The subject line follows the 'type(scope): subject' conventional-commit format
The subject line has no trailing period
There is a Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer
The message has a body (more than just a subject line)
The body explains WHY the change was made (the auto-cover-future-Oracle-scenarios rationale), not just what changed
Code identifiers like oracleImage and strings.Contains are wrapped in backticks
The subject line is in imperative mood (e.g. 'add'/'use', not 'added'/'uses')

scenarios.name: terse-request-still-needs-body
scenarios.prompt: Quick one — just give me a commit message for fixing a typo in the README where 'recieve' should be 'receive'.

assertions.text: The subject follows the 'type(scope): subject' format
There is a Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer even though the request was terse
There is a body explaining the change, not only a subject line

scenarios.name: noisy-context
scenarios.prompt:
Here is some context from our debugging session earlier:

[10:02] build passed on CI run 4821
[10:03] flaky test FileAssessmentsTable.spec retried twice then passed
[10:05] discussed whether to memoize the selector, decided not to for now
[10:10] noticed the API returns 409 when a resource is in use
[10:14] lunch

Anyway, back to the task: I just added a null check to the FileAssessmentsTable component so it renders an empty state instead of crashing when the assessments API returns an empty response. Write me the commit message. Scope it to MIG-7922.

assertions.text: The subject follows the 'type(scope): subject' format
There is a Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer
The body explains why (guarding against the empty/null API response), not the unrelated debugging-log chatter
The message does not dump the irrelevant CI/lunch context into the commit body

scenarios.name: fixup-amend-on-branch
scenarios.prompt: I found a bug: the FileAssessmentsTable null check I wrote is wrong. That table was added earlier on THIS branch (not merged yet). I want to fold this fix into the commit that introduced the table, not make a new standalone commit. The fix touches onprem/ui/src/.../FileAssessmentsTable.tsx. Walk me through the exact git commands you'd run.

assertions.text: Uses `git absorb` to attribute the staged fix to the right branch commit
Does not use `git add -A` or `git add .` (stages specific files by name)
Relies on git absorb for target attribution rather than hand-picking a SHA via git blame or eyeballing the log
Stages the specific file by name before running absorb, and treats absorb as only seeing staged hunks
Creates a fixup commit rather than directly running git commit --amend on the target commit
Mentions inspecting what absorb produced (e.g. git log / git status) and handling any hunks absorb could not attribute
